ayush_description: Disorder caused by upward movement of vata
reason: Udavarta in Ayurveda refers to the upward or abnormal movement of Vata, often due to the suppression of natural bodily urges (like flatus, defecation, hunger, thirst, or vomiting). This 'upward movement of vata' frequently manifests as symptoms in the gastrointestinal tract, such as abdominal distension, bloating, gas, heartburn, and general discomfort in the upper abdomen. The ICD-11 term 'Dyspepsia,' defined as 'Indigestion and upper abdominal discomfort,' directly aligns with these common manifestations of Udavarta caused by the upward movement of Vata in the digestive system. While Udavarta can have other manifestations (e.g., urinary issues), dyspepsia is one of the most direct and common symptomatic outcomes implied by the 'upward movement of vata' description among the given options.
